Package org.eventb.core

Interface IPOSequent

All Superinterfaces:
IAccuracyElement, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IElementManipulation, IInternalElement, IParent, IPOStampedElement, IRodinElement, ISnapshotable
All Known Implementing Classes:
POSequent
public interface IPOSequent extends IInternalElement, IPOStampedElement, IAccuracyElement
Common protocol for proof obligations in Event-B Proof Obligation (PO) files.

A sequent is a tuple (NAME, TYPE_ENV, HYP, GOAL, HINTS) consists of

  • a name (String)
  • a hypothesis (IPOPredicateSet)
  • a goal (IPOPredicate)
  • some hints (IPOHint[])
  • some handles to the source from which this proof obligation is derived (IPOSource[])

Since:
1.0

  • Field Details

    • ELEMENT_TYPE

      static final IInternalElementType<IPOSequent> ELEMENT_TYPE

  • Method Details

    • getHypothesis

      IPOPredicateSet getHypothesis(String elementName)
      Returns a handle to a child hypothesis (predicate set) with the given element name.

      This is a handle-only method. The child element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      elementName - element name of the hypthesis
      Returns:
      a handle to a child hypthesis with the given element name

    • getHypotheses

      IPOPredicateSet[] getHypotheses() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the predicate sets containing the hypotheses of this proof obligation. In the current version, only one predicate set is stored by the POG. Tools can then consider it as an error if the returned array has a length different from 1.
      Returns:
      an array of predicate sets containing the hypotheses of this proof obligation
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • getGoal

      IPOPredicate getGoal(String elementName)
      Returns a handle to a child goal (predicate) with the given element name.

      This is a handle-only method. The child element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      elementName - element name of the goal
      Returns:
      a handle to a child goal with the given element name

    • getGoals

      IPOPredicate[] getGoals() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the goal predicates of this proof obligation. In the current version, only one goal predicate is stored by the POG. Tools can then consider it as an error if the returned array has a length different from 1.
      Returns:
      an array of goal predicates of this proof obligation
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • getDescription

      String getDescription() throws RodinDBException
      Returns a more descriptive name of this proof obligation.

      Equivalent to getPOGNature().getDescription(). Use getPOGNature() to compare Proof Obligation natures.

      Returns:
      a descriptive proof obligation name
      Throws:
      RodinDBException

    • getSource

      IPOSource getSource(String elementName)
      Returns a handle to a child source with the given element name.

      This is a handle-only method. The child element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      elementName - element name of the source
      Returns:
      a handle to a child source with the given element name

    • getSources

      IPOSource[] getSources() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the (most important) source elements of a proof obligation.

      The returned elements contain handle identifiers to elements of the database.

      Returns:
      the array of sources associated with the proof obligation that contains this description
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • getSelectionHint

      IPOSelectionHint getSelectionHint(String elementName)
      Returns a handle to a child hint with the given element name.

      This is a handle-only method. The child element may or may not be present.

      Parameters:
      elementName - element name of the hint
      Returns:
      a handle to a child hint with the given element name

    • getSelectionHints

      IPOSelectionHint[] getSelectionHints() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the hints for a proof obligation.
      Returns:
      the array of hints associated with the proof obligation that contains this description
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • getPOGNature

      IPOGNature getPOGNature() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the nature of this proof obligation.
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database
      Since:
      1.3

    • setPOGNature

      void setPOGNature(IPOGNature nature, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets the nature of this proof obligation. Actually, IPOSequent.setPOGNature(nature, monitor) and IPOSequent.setDescription(nature.getDescription(), monitor) are equivalent.
      Parameters:
      nature - the nature
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database
      Since:
      1.3
